content:
Korean team is currently working on a our own project spreadsheet.
When we make pull request, we first organize all the changes since last version into one csv file, and upload that file.

My question is, what happens if i delete previous files, which are already compiled into game?

For example, there was a file named "KO_Translate_251013.csv", and it is applied to game version 2.3.4.
And if i upload a file "KO_Translate_251110.csv", when next compile happens, will the previous file (251013.csv) be applied again?

The reason why i am asking this is, the new file(251110.csv) only contains the changes, but it shares some same keys in previous uploads with different translation. (correcting mistranslation, etc.)
If the files are compiled in a chronological or alphabetical order, it is okay (251013.csv -> 251110.csv).
But if it is compiled in a random order (like 251110.csv -> 251013.csv), or it may cause some kind of conflict, it could be a problem.

If i delete previous files (already compiled ones), will the texts be reverted to ML TLs? 
Or deleting previous files doesn't affect currently compiled texts?

I don't even know what i am saying but i want to make sure. Thank you.
thread_title: Deleting previous, already compiled csv files
Deleting previous, already compiled csv files
